questions: Should the `taskParamsUtils.ts` display/analysis functions (used by UI to parse task params for display in TasksPane, SegmentCard, etc.) remain client-side or also move? They read FROM task params, not write them, so likely stay — but confirming since the file references task-family-specific field names.

For the travel orchestrator's `ensureShotParentGenerationId()` — this calls a DB RPC that may CREATE a generation record. Moving this to the edge function means generation creation happens server-side. Is that acceptable, or should the client still handle parent generation creation before calling the edge function?

The `generateVideoService.ts` in the travel tool does complex pre-processing (fetching fresh shot_generations from DB, building image payloads from timeline/batch/by-pair modes, resolving model specs) that is UI-state-dependent. How much of this orchestration should move server-side vs. remain as client-side pre-processing that feeds into the resolver input?
Should the new API support the old raw `{ params, task_type }` request shape permanently (for external API/PAT consumers) or only as a transitional bridge during migration?

For batch responses, should the edge function return `parentGenerationId` (needed by travel between images callers like `generateVideoService.ts`) in the response, or should callers that need it query for it after task creation?
success_criteria: TypeScript compiles with zero errors after all client-side param-building code is deleted
Every resolver produces task params byte-identical (or semantically equivalent) to the pre-migration client-side output for the same input — verified by snapshot comparison
Batch requests create exactly N task rows from a single HTTP request (verified in DB)
All 10+ task families work end-to-end: UI action → edge function resolver → correct DB row → worker processes successfully → generation appears in UI
No client-side file in `src/shared/lib/tasks/families/`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/`, or `src/shared/lib/tasks/imageEditing/` exists after cleanup
Edge function cold start time does not regress by more than 500ms (measured via Supabase logs)
Resolution calculation produces identical results server-side (verified by logging both client and server results during migration overlap)
The `create-task` edge function handles validation errors with clear 400 responses including field-level error details (matching current client-side TaskValidationError behavior)
assumptions: '6 task families' refers to the 6 directories in `src/shared/lib/tasks/families/` (imageGeneration, imageUpscale, individualTravelSegment, joinClips, videoEnhance, zImageTurboI2I). Non-family creators (travelBetweenImages, magicEdit, maskedEdit, crossfadeJoin) are also in scope per 'ALL task creation logic'.
No backward compatibility needed means we can change the `create-task` request schema and deploy client+server together (no rolling deploy concern).
'Batch support inside edge function' means a single HTTP request can create multiple tasks (array insert), replacing the current client-side Promise.allSettled pattern.
'Travel orchestrator creates only orchestrator task' describes the current behavior (it already creates one task). The change is that payload building moves server-side.
Workers are NOT changed — resolver output must produce params blobs identical to what workers currently expect.
The Deno edge function runtime can handle the additional resolver code without hitting memory/timeout limits (current function is ~250 lines; will grow to ~3000+ lines across resolver files).
Client-side UI state processing (e.g., timeline mode → image pairs in generateVideoService.ts) stays client-side; only the param building/validation/resolution/ID generation moves.

The `taskPayloadContract.ts` and `taskFamilyContracts.ts` contract system (used by joinClips and travelBetweenImages) will be ported to server-side shared code since these contracts are part of the param-building pipeline.
External API consumers using PATs currently send raw `{ params, task_type }` — the old path can be removed since the user said no backward compat needed, but this affects any external integrations.
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
